texto: She read her way around the library, hungry for journeys, adventures, laughter and passion. She took each new book to bed like a lover, savouring every chapter, going too far some nights until the letters danced like insects and she was groggy next day at work. But still she'd sneak away for lunchtime trysts, her eager fingers fumbling for the bookmark.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cath Staincliffe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “She read her way around the library, hungry for journeys, adventures, laughter and passion. She t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, books, libraries pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e viagem.
